{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Support Officer",
  "description": "Wragby Business Solutions &amp; Technologies Limited (2016 Microsoft Partner of the Year for Cloud Platform in Nigeria) is a cutting edge end-to-end business technology services and solutions provider. We empower our customers to fully optimize day-to-day operational tasks by providing best-in-class business and cloud solutions, technology solutions, embrace innovation, reduce risk and further control costs. We design, implement and manage IT systems to enable organizations that rely on IT to grow and flourish. We provide services to manage our customers&#39;? entire IT infrastructures from servers and desktops to applications, data and networks.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support Officer
Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria
Employment Type: Full-Time

About the Role


	We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Support Officer to provide first-line and second-line technical support across the organization.
	The ideal candidate will ensure the smooth operation of end-user devices, business applications, networks, and IT infrastructure while delivering excellent user support and maintaining high service standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide technical support for hardware, software, network, and business applications.
	Install, configure, troubleshoot, and maintain desktops, laptops, printers, and other IT equipment.
	Support Microsoft 365, Microsoft Teams, user accounts, and enterprise applications.
	Resolve user incidents and service requests within agreed Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
	Maintain accurate IT asset records and inventory.
	Support employee onboarding and offboarding, including account provisioning and equipment deployment.
	Perform routine system maintenance, software updates, and endpoint security checks.
	Troubleshoot network connectivity, VPN, Wi-Fi, and printing issues.
	Escalate complex technical issues where necessary and ensure timely resolution.
	Promote cybersecurity awareness and provide end-user training on IT best practices.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3&ndash;4 years of hands-on experience in IT Support or a similar technical support role.
	Strong knowledge of Windows OS, Microsoft 365, networking, hardware troubleshooting, and IT Service Management.
	Experience supporting enterprise applications and maintaining IT assets.
	Excellent problem-solving, communication, and customer service skills.
	Professional certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft 365 Fundamentals (MS-900), or ITIL Foundation will be an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to cv@wragbysolutions.com using &quot;IT Support Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-08-12",

IT Support Officer Job at Wragby Business Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Wragby Business Solutions & Technologies Limited (2016 Microsoft Partner of the Year for Cloud Platform in Nigeria) is a cutting edge end-to-end business technology services and solutions provider. We empower our customers to fully optimize day-to-day operational tasks by providing best-in-class business and cloud solutions, technology solutions, embrace innovation, reduce risk and further control costs. We design, implement and manage IT systems to enable organizations that rely on IT to grow and flourish. We provide services to manage our customers'? entire IT infrastructures from servers and desktops to applications, data and networks.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

            
    



Job Position: IT Support Officer
Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria
Employment Type: Full-Time

About the Role


	We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Support Officer to provide first-line and second-line technical support across the organization.
	The ideal candidate will ensure the smooth operation of end-user devices, business applications, networks, and IT infrastructure while delivering excellent user support and maintaining high service standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide technical support for hardware, software, network, and business applications.
	Install, configure, troubleshoot, and maintain desktops, laptops, printers, and other IT equipment.
	Support Microsoft 365, Microsoft Teams, user accounts, and enterprise applications.
	Resolve user incidents and service requests within agreed Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
	Maintain accurate IT asset records and inventory.
	Support employee onboarding and offboarding, including account provisioning and equipment deployment.
	Perform routine system maintenance, software updates, and endpoint security checks.
	Troubleshoot network connectivity, VPN, Wi-Fi, and printing issues.
	Escalate complex technical issues where necessary and ensure timely resolution.
	Promote cybersecurity awareness and provide end-user training on IT best practices.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3–4 years of hands-on experience in IT Support or a similar technical support role.
	Strong knowledge of Windows OS, Microsoft 365, networking, hardware troubleshooting, and IT Service Management.
	Experience supporting enterprise applications and maintaining IT assets.
	Excellent problem-solving, communication, and customer service skills.
	Professional certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft 365 Fundamentals (MS-900), or ITIL Foundation will be an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using "IT Support Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Deck Engineer",
  "description": "Red Offshore Nigeria was incorporated in 2008 as a pure ship brokerage company. Over the years, our expansion brought vessel management, crewing, and asset acquisition to our growing track record.

Today, we are a 100% Nigerian-owned, fully integrated indigenous operator recognised and certified by every major regulatory authority in our operating regions. We pride ourselves on complete transparency &mdash; no hidden costs, no grey areas.

We are currently recruiting experienced offshore personnel for an ongoing project in Nigeria in the capacity below:

Job Position: Deck Engineer

Project Ref.: 10286
Job Location: Nigeria
Employment Type: Full-Time

Requirements


	Relevant offshore and marine industry experience
	Valid STCW certification (mandatory)
	Valid BOSIET and HUET certification (preferred)


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to crewing@redoffshore.eu using &quot;Nigeria &ndash; Project Ref. 10286&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Deck Engineer Job at Red Offshore Nigeria




        
    

        
        Red Offshore Nigeria was incorporated in 2008 as a pure ship brokerage company. Over the years, our expansion brought vessel management, crewing, and asset acquisition to our growing track record.

Today, we are a 100% Nigerian-owned, fully integrated indigenous operator recognised and certified by every major regulatory authority in our operating regions. We pride ourselves on complete transparency — no hidden costs, no grey areas.

We are currently recruiting experienced offshore personnel for an ongoing project in Nigeria in the capacity below:

Job Position: Deck Engineer

Project Ref.: 10286
Job Location: Nigeria
Employment Type: Full-Time

Requirements
     

            
    




	Relevant offshore and marine industry experience
	Valid STCW certification (mandatory)
	Valid BOSIET and HUET certification (preferred)


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using "Nigeria – Project Ref. 10286" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Warehouse Supervisor",
  "description": "NutriK FZE, a proud subsidiary of the globally recognized Nutriset Group, plays a vital role in producing and distributing high?quality ready?to?use foods that support the treatment and prevention of malnutrition across Nigeria. Our mission is driven by purpose: to help save lives and improve the well?being of children and families in need. We specialize in the production of essential nutritional products including Ready?to?Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF), Ready?to?Use Supplementary Food (RUSF), and Lipid?Based Nutrient Supplements (LNS) which support humanitarian and health interventions nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Supervisor
Job Location: Kano

General objective


	To strengthen our growing operations in Kano, NutriK is seeking a dedicated and experienced Warehouse Supervisor to join our Production Department.
	The Warehouse Supervisor is responsible for overseeing and coordinating all warehouse operations at NutriK FZE to ensure the safe, efficient, and compliant storage, handling, and distribution of raw materials, packaging materials, and finished products. The role ensures inventory accuracy, food safety compliance, and timely support to production and dispatch operations.


Responsibilities


	Supervise daily warehouse operations including receiving, storage, picking, packing, and dispatch of goods
	Ensure proper handling and storage of food products in compliance with food safety, hygiene, and quality standards (FIFO/FEFO)
	Coordinate with Production to ensure uninterrupted supply of raw and packaging materials
	Oversee loading and unloading activities to ensure safety and product integrity
	Maintain accurate inventory records in the ERP or stock management system
	Conduct regular stock counts and reconcile physical stock with system records
	Identify and report stock discrepancies, damages, or losses
	Ensure warehouse cleanliness, organization, and pest?free conditions at all times
	Implement and enforce warehouse safety rules and procedures
	Supervise, train, and evaluate warehouse staff performance
	Prepare warehouse reports (stock levels, expiries, movements, and KPIs)
	Coordinate with Logistics for timely and accurate dispatch of finished goods
	Support internal and external audits related to inventory and food safety
	Participate in continuous improvement initiatives related to warehouse efficiency
	Any other responsibility assigns by line manager.


Required Skills:


	Language skills: English.
	Work experience &ndash; 3 &ndash; 5 years of relevant experience in warehouse or logistics experience, preferably in food manufacturing.
	Education Degree &ndash; Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Food Science, or related field.
	Computer skills &ndash; MS Word, MS Excel &amp; Outlook


Other required skills:


	Demonstrated leadership and strong performance in inventory management
	Good understanding of food safety and quality standards
	Proven experience supervising warehouses in food manufacturing or FMCG environment
	Strong knowledge of inventory control and food safety requirements
	Warehouse operations and inventory management principles
	Food safety standards (HACCP, GMP, hygiene regulations)
	FIFO / FEFO stock management practices
	Occupational health and safety requirements.


Note


	Only candidates who meet the selection criteria will be contacted.
	This position is open to Nigerian nationals only.

Warehouse Supervisor Job at NutriK FZE




        
    

        
        NutriK FZE, a proud subsidiary of the globally recognized Nutriset Group, plays a vital role in producing and distributing high?quality ready?to?use foods that support the treatment and prevention of malnutrition across Nigeria. Our mission is driven by purpose: to help save lives and improve the well?being of children and families in need. We specialize in the production of essential nutritional products including Ready?to?Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF), Ready?to?Use Supplementary Food (RUSF), and Lipid?Based Nutrient Supplements (LNS) which support humanitarian and health interventions nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Supervisor
Job Location: Kano
     

            
    



General objective


	To strengthen our growing operations in Kano, NutriK is seeking a dedicated and experienced Warehouse Supervisor to join our Production Department.
	The Warehouse Supervisor is responsible for overseeing and coordinating all warehouse operations at NutriK FZE to ensure the safe, efficient, and compliant storage, handling, and distribution of raw materials, packaging materials, and finished products. The role ensures inventory accuracy, food safety compliance, and timely support to production and dispatch operations.


Responsibilities


	Supervise daily warehouse operations including receiving, storage, picking, packing, and dispatch of goods
	Ensure proper handling and storage of food products in compliance with food safety, hygiene, and quality standards (FIFO/FEFO)
	Coordinate with Production to ensure uninterrupted supply of raw and packaging materials
	Oversee loading and unloading activities to ensure safety and product integrity
	Maintain accurate inventory records in the ERP or stock management system
	Conduct regular stock counts and reconcile physical stock with system records
	Identify and report stock discrepancies, damages, or losses
	Ensure warehouse cleanliness, organization, and pest?free conditions at all times
	Implement and enforce warehouse safety rules and procedures
	Supervise, train, and evaluate warehouse staff performance
	Prepare warehouse reports (stock levels, expiries, movements, and KPIs)
	Coordinate with Logistics for timely and accurate dispatch of finished goods
	Support internal and external audits related to inventory and food safety
	Participate in continuous improvement initiatives related to warehouse efficiency
	Any other responsibility assigns by line manager.


Required Skills:


	Language skills: English.
	Work experience – 3 – 5 years of relevant experience in warehouse or logistics experience, preferably in food manufacturing.
	Education Degree – Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Food Science, or related field.
	Computer skills – MS Word, MS Excel & Outlook


Other required skills:


	Demonstrated leadership and strong performance in inventory management
	Good understanding of food safety and quality standards
	Proven experience supervising warehouses in food manufacturing or FMCG environment
	Strong knowledge of inventory control and food safety requirements
	Warehouse operations and inventory management principles
	Food safety standards (HACCP, GMP, hygiene regulations)
	FIFO / FEFO stock management practices
	Occupational health and safety requirements.


Note


	Only candidates who meet the selection criteria will be contacted.
	This position is open to Nigerian nationals only.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Bolton White Group - We are a group of&nbsp;&nbsp;hospitality brands providing exceptional services across all our business units. Our team is dedicated to providing a wide range of clients with world-class services. Bolton White Group is well regarded for its focus on international best practices in all aspects of its business.&nbsp;

Bolton White Group is an independent entrepreneurial-driven institution offering a complete range of hospitality services with a focus on providing a &lsquo;home away from home&rsquo; experience to our clients. By choosing to join Bolton White Group you have assumed your share of the responsibility for maintaining the high principles and standards to which we are dedicated.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Hotel Cashier&nbsp;

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Handle guest check-ins/check-outs, billing, and payment transactions accurately.
	Process cash, credit card, debit card, and other payment methods in compliance with hotel policies.
	Issue receipts, invoices, and refunds when necessary.
	Maintain accurate cash drawers and reconcile transactions at the beginning and end of shifts.
	Assist in preparing daily cashier reports, deposits, and financial records.
	Respond to guest inquiries about charges, payments, and billing issues.
	Support front office and F&amp;B teams in handling guest accounts and room charges.
	Follow internal control procedures to prevent fraud and ensure compliance with financial standards.
	Maintain the confidentiality of guest and financial information.
	Provide courteous and professional service to all guests.


Qualifications &amp; Skills


	Minimum of a B.Sc. in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Previous experience as a cashier, teller, or in a finance role; hospitality experience preferred.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in using POS systems, hotel management software (e.g., Opera, Micros), and MS Office.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills.
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.
	Ability to work flexible shifts, weekends, and holidays.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Photograph to: jobs@boltonwhitegroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

Hotel Cashier Job at Bolton White Group




        
    

        
        Bolton White Group - We are a group of  hospitality brands providing exceptional services across all our business units. Our team is dedicated to providing a wide range of clients with world-class services. Bolton White Group is well regarded for its focus on international best practices in all aspects of its business. 

Bolton White Group is an independent entrepreneurial-driven institution offering a complete range of hospitality services with a focus on providing a ‘home away from home’ experience to our clients. By choosing to join Bolton White Group you have assumed your share of the responsibility for maintaining the high principles and standards to which we are dedicated.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:
     

            
    



 Job Position: Hotel Cashier 

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Handle guest check-ins/check-outs, billing, and payment transactions accurately.
	Process cash, credit card, debit card, and other payment methods in compliance with hotel policies.
	Issue receipts, invoices, and refunds when necessary.
	Maintain accurate cash drawers and reconcile transactions at the beginning and end of shifts.
	Assist in preparing daily cashier reports, deposits, and financial records.
	Respond to guest inquiries about charges, payments, and billing issues.
	Support front office and F&B teams in handling guest accounts and room charges.
	Follow internal control procedures to prevent fraud and ensure compliance with financial standards.
	Maintain the confidentiality of guest and financial information.
	Provide courteous and professional service to all guests.


Qualifications & Skills


	Minimum of a B.Sc. in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Previous experience as a cashier, teller, or in a finance role; hospitality experience preferred.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in using POS systems, hotel management software (e.g., Opera, Micros), and MS Office.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills.
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.
	Ability to work flexible shifts, weekends, and holidays.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Photograph to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply
